reasoning: The 10-year breakeven inflation rate on the last trading day of August 2025 (August 29) was 2.41%. This represents a relatively high point compared to the historical range over the past year (trading mostly between 2.10% and 2.43%). Because the resolution criteria require the rate to have strictly increased (i.e., >2.41%), and mean reversion makes a decrease or sideways movement more likely from this elevated level, the probability of a strict increase is below 50%. The FRED statistical trend analysis yields a probability of roughly 0.35 for an increase by September 7, given short-term downward momentum and seasonal factors. As the forecast horizon extends (from 1 month out to 6 months out), uncertainty grows and mean reversion applies more weakly, pushing the probability of exceeding the target threshold closer to 0.5. Thus, the estimates are stepped from 0.35 up to 0.48 over the four resolution dates.
